description:
You are an automation-workflow specialist who has just received two small CSV data extracts that were deposited in the shared data area:

• /home/user/data/employees.csv  
• /home/user/data/salaries.csv  

Your goal is to prove that you can manipulate, join, filter, and convert structured data entirely from the Linux command line.

Phase 1 – Workspace preparation  
1. Create a new directory /home/user/output.  
2. Everything the rest of this task produces must be placed inside /home/user/output.

Phase 2 – Join and filter the data  
1. Read both CSV files.  
   a. employees.csv has the exact header line:  
      id,name,department,hire_date  
   b. salaries.csv has the exact header line:  
      id,year,amount  
2. Perform a join on the id column so that each employee is matched with the corresponding salary record(s).  
3. Filter the joined rows so that only salaries for the year 2022 that are strictly greater than 70000 remain.

Phase 3 – Generate two deliverables  
A. JSON deliverable (/home/user/output/high_earners_2022.json)  
   • The file must be valid JSON.  
   • It must contain a single top-level array.  
   • Every array element must be an object with the following properties, in exactly this order:  
     1. id (number)  
     2. name (string)  
     3. department (string)  
     4. hire_date (string, ISO-8601 yyyy-mm-dd)  
     5. amount (number, the 2022 salary matched in Phase 2)  
   • The objects must be sorted in ascending lexical order of the “name” field.  
   • Do not include any additional keys, whitespace, or trailing commas.

B. Summary CSV deliverable (/home/user/output/department_summary_2022.csv)  
   • This file must start with the following exact header line:  
     department,employee_count,total_salary  
   • Each subsequent line must contain:  
     • department (from employees.csv)  
     • employee_count – number of employees from that department present in the Phase 2 filtered set  
     • total_salary – sum of their 2022 salaries (integer, no decimals)  
   • Lines must be sorted in descending order of total_salary.  
   • Departments with zero qualifying employees must NOT appear.

Phase 4 – Audit log  
Create /home/user/output/workflow.log capturing, line-by-line, the major actions you performed.  
Required exact format for every line:  
YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS – ACTION_DESCRIPTION  
Use 24-hour time, local system timezone, and keep ACTION_DESCRIPTION short (e.g., “Joined data”, “Filtered >70000”, “Wrote JSON”, etc.).  
A minimum of four log lines are expected (directory creation, join, filter, outputs written).

Verification notes for the automated tests  
• All three output files must exist at the exact paths specified above.  
• Path and filename spelling is case-sensitive.  
• The JSON must parse cleanly with jq -e . /home/user/output/high_earners_2022.json.  
• The CSV summary must have Unix LF line endings and no trailing blank lines.  
• The workflow.log must contain at least four timestamped lines matching the prescribed pattern.
truth:
Pre-existing input files (already present before the agent starts):

1. /home/user/data/employees.csv  
------------------------------------------------  
id,name,department,hire_date  
1,Alice Zephyr,Engineering,2019-03-14  
2,Bob Yellow,Sales,2018-07-22  
3,Charlie Xander,Engineering,2020-11-03  
4,Diana White,HR,2017-01-30  
5,Edward Violet,Engineering,2016-05-12  
6,Frida Umber,Sales,2021-09-08  

2. /home/user/data/salaries.csv  
------------------------------------------------  
id,year,amount  
1,2021,68000  
1,2022,72000  
2,2022,69000  
3,2022,88000  
4,2022,65000  
5,2021,70000  
5,2022,91000  
6,2022,73000  

Expected filtered join result (year 2022 & amount > 70000):

id name            department   hire_date   amount  
1  Alice Zephyr    Engineering  2019-03-14  72000  
3  Charlie Xander  Engineering  2020-11-03  88000  
5  Edward Violet   Engineering  2016-05-12  91000  
6  Frida Umber     Sales        2021-09-08  73000  

Expected /home/user/output/high_earners_2022.json  
------------------------------------------------  
[  
{"id":1,"name":"Alice Zephyr","department":"Engineering","hire_date":"2019-03-14","amount":72000},  
{"id":3,"name":"Charlie Xander","department":"Engineering","hire_date":"2020-11-03","amount":88000},  
{"id":5,"name":"Edward Violet","department":"Engineering","hire_date":"2016-05-12","amount":91000},  
{"id":6,"name":"Frida Umber","department":"Sales","hire_date":"2021-09-08","amount":73000}  
]

Expected /home/user/output/department_summary_2022.csv  
------------------------------------------------  
department,employee_count,total_salary  
Engineering,3,251000  
Sales,1,73000  

workflow.log must contain at least four lines that individually match the regex:  
^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} – .+$

Directory /home/user/output must be created with default user-writable permissions (umask 002 or similar).
